instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.explanation: The biological name of an organism consists of the genus name and species name. the correct biological nomenclature of Mango is Mangifer indica Linn.

questions.question: Which one of these is not a eukaryote?
questions.question_raw: Which one of these is not a eukaryote?
questions.explanation: Anabaena is not a eukaryote but a prokaryote.

questions.question: The megasporangium of naked seed plants are called
questions.question_raw: The megasporangium of naked seed plants are called
questions.explanation: The cones bearing megasporophylls with ovules or megasporangia are called macrosporangiate or female strobili.

questions.question: Which two teeth are modified into tusk in elephants?
questions.question_raw: Which two teeth are modified into tusk in elephants?
questions.explanation: In elephants, two upper front teeth are modified into tusk of elephants. The continuous growth of tusk is enabled by formative tissues in the apical opening of the root of the teeth.

questions.question: In which class of fungi the mushroom belongs to:
questions.question_raw: In which class of fungi the mushroom belongs to:
questions.explanation: The mushroom belongs to class Basidiomycetes to kingdom fungi. Basidiomycetes produce basidiospores as reproductive units. The member of this group grows on dead and decaying matters.

questions.question: Squamous epithelium is found in which of the following places?
questions.question_raw: Squamous epithelium is found in which of the following places?
questions.explanation: The squamous epithelium forms a thin flat layer. So they are found in places where exchange or diffusion of material takes place, such as the lining of blood vessels.

questions.question: Which of the following features is common to prokaryotes and many eukaryotes?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following features is common to prokaryotes and many eukaryotes?
questions.explanation: Cell wall is found in plant cell either of prokaryotic or eukaryotic cell.

questions.question: Chitin occurs in the cell wall of _______.
questions.question_raw: Chitin occurs in the cell wall of _______.
questions.explanation: The cell wall of fungi is made of chitin, complex sugar molecules. The cell wall of plants is made up of cellulose.

questions.question: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.question_raw: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.explanation: The shapes of bacteria are bacillus (rod-like), coccus (spherical), vibrio (comma-shaped) and spirillum (spiral).

questions.question: Dietary proteins are otherwise called as:
questions.question_raw: Dietary proteins are otherwise called as:
questions.explanation: Dietary proteins are the source of essential amino acids. Therefore, amino acids can be essential or non-essential. The latter are those which our body can make, while we get essential amino acids through our diet.

questions.question: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.question_raw: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.explanation: Houseflies are insects of the order Diptera, the name is derived from the Greek di = two, and ptera = wings

questions.question: The number of substrate molecules changed per minute by a molecule of the enzyme is called:
questions.question_raw: The number of substrate molecules changed per minute by a molecule of the enzyme is called:
questions.explanation: The number of substrate molecules changed per minute by a molecule of the enzyme is called turn over number. Higher the turn over number more effective is the enzyme.

questions.question: Male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence in_____.
questions.question_raw: Male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence in_____.
questions.explanation: Unlike bryophytes and pteridophytes, in gymnosperms (Cedrus) the male and the female gametophytes do not have an independent free-living existence.

questions.question: Identify the algae present in the figure
questions.question_raw: Identify the algae present in the figure
questions.explanation: The algae present in the figure is Chlamydomonas which is a unicellular algae present in fresh water. Two flagella is attached to it that helps in the movement in water.

questions.question: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell know can survive without oxygen is_______.
questions.question_raw: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell know can survive without oxygen is_______.
questions.explanation: Mycoplasmas are a group of organisms that lack a cell wall and are the smallest living organisms. They can survive without oxygen. Mycoplasmas are also called as PPLO (pleuro pneumonia-like organism).

questions.question: Collagen is:
questions.question_raw: Collagen is:
questions.explanation: In all connective tissues except blood, the cells secrete fibres of structural proteins called collagen or elastin.

questions.question: The chromosome having centromeres at one terminal end is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The chromosome having centromeres at one terminal end is called ________.
questions.explanation: A telocentric chromosome's centromere is located at the terminal end of the chromosome. Telomeres may extend from both ends of the chromosome their shape is like I during anaphase. For example, the standard house mouse karyotype has only telocentric chromosomes. Humans do not possess telocentric chromosomes.

questions.question: The sequence of amino acids in a protein is called:
questions.question_raw: The sequence of amino acids in a protein is called:
questions.explanation: The amino acid sequence of a particular protein determines its shape also called conformation. The organization of protein structure is divided into four levels, called primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary. The primary structure of a protein is simply the sequence of amino acids.

questions.question: Herbarium is:
questions.question_raw: Herbarium is:

options.text: Collection of preserved plant and animal specimen
Collection of living plants for reference

questions.explanation: The herbarium is a storehouse of collected plant specimens that are dried, pressed and preserved on sheets. Hence, the correct option is Collection of dried plant specimens.

questions.question: The number of ganglia in the nervous system of cockroach is:
questions.question_raw: The number of ganglia in the nervous system of cockroach is:

options.text: 3 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen
4 in thorax and 3 in the abdomen
6 in thorax and 3 in the abdomen
4 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen

correct_option_texts: 3 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen
questions.explanation: The nervous system of cockroach consists of a series of fused, segmentally arranged ganglia. They are joined by paired longitudinal connectives on the ventral side. Three ganglia lie in the thorax, and six ganglia are found in the abdomen.

questions.question: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by _____________.
questions.question_raw: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by _____________.
questions.explanation: The fusion of two polar nuclei with one male gamete is called triple fusion. The primary endosperm nucleus is formed by triple fusion and divide mitotically to form endosperm.

questions.question: Urecotelic excretion is found in which of the following pairs of animals?
questions.question_raw: Urecotelic excretion is found in which of the following pairs of animals?
questions.explanation: Lizards and Sparrow are urecotelic. They release highly concentrated uric acid as an excretory product.

questions.question: Plant disease in which pathogen is seen as cottony growth on the surface of the host is:
questions.question_raw: Plant disease in which pathogen is seen as cottony growth on the surface of the host is:
questions.explanation: Downy mildew disease is caused due to fungus that forms cottony growth on the surface of the host. The leaves' surface gets reduced that alters the photosynthesis rate.

questions.question: Muscle forming cells are called:
questions.question_raw: Muscle forming cells are called:
questions.explanation: The muscle forming cells are called myoblasts cells. Myoblast is a type of embryonic progenitor cell that differentiates to give rise to muscle cells.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The living state is a non-equilibrium steady-state to be able to perform work.
Reason (R): The living process is a constant effort to prevent falling into equilibrium and is achieved by energy input.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The living state is a non-equilibrium steady-state to be able to perform work.
Reason (R): The living process is a constant effort to prevent falling into equilibrium and is achieved by energy input.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: As living organisms work continuously, they cannot afford to reach equilibrium. Hence the living state is a non-equilibrium steady-state to be able to perform work; living process is a constant effort to prevent falling into equilibrium. This is achieved by energy input.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Adjacent monosaccharides are held by glycosidic bonds.
Reason (R): Monosaccharides are macromolecules.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Adjacent monosaccharides are held by glycosidic bonds.
Reason (R): Monosaccharides are macromolecules.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Monosaccharides are micromolecules. Monosaccharides are unit of carbohydrates. Adjacent monosaccharides are held by glycosidic bonds and if they are two, they form disaccharides and if they are more than two, they form pilysaccharides.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Lipids present in the outer and inner side of the bilayer membrane are mostly the same.
Reason (R): Oligosaccharides are attached to the external surface as well as the inner surface of a biomembrane.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Lipids present in the outer and inner side of the bilayer membrane are mostly the same.
Reason (R): Oligosaccharides are attached to the external surface as well as the inner surface of a biomembrane.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: A is true but R is false.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Juvenile erect leafy gametophyte of Sphagnum is called gametophore.
Reason (R): Gametophores bear rhizoids like the mature gametophyte.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Juvenile erect leafy gametophyte of Sphagnum is called gametophore.
Reason (R): Gametophores bear rhizoids like the mature gametophyte.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation:
From the margin of the lobed protonema arises the erect, leafy gametophyte called the Sphagnum plant. It is also called the gametophore. The stem of the young gametophore bears a few colourless rhizoids at its base. As the gametophore grows, the rhizoids soon disappear. The mature gametophytes of sphagnum thus do not possess rhizoids.
Fig. Sphagnum. Juvenile stage.

questions.question: Which phylum has a water vascular system as a characteristic feature?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has a water vascular system as a characteristic feature?
questions.explanation: The most distinctive feature of Echinodermata is the presence of the water vascular system.

questions.question: All eukaryotic unicellular organisms belong to
questions.question_raw: All eukaryotic unicellular organisms belong to
questions.explanation: Monera contains all prokaryotic organisms and bacteria. Fungi can have unicellular as well as multicellular organisms that comes under the Protista.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct definition of tissue?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is the correct definition of tissue?

options.text: Group of similar cells formed to carry out a variety of functions.
Group of similar cells formed to carry out similar functions.
Group of a variety of cells formed to carry out a variety of functions.
Group of a variety of cells formed to carry out a similar function.

correct_option_texts: Group of similar cells formed to carry out similar functions.
questions.explanation: Group of similar cells formed to carry out similar functions.

questions.question: The number of ends in a glycogen molecule would be:
questions.question_raw: The number of ends in a glycogen molecule would be:

options.text: Equal to the number of branches plus one
Equal to the number of branch points
Two, one on the left side and another on the right side

correct_option_texts: Equal to the number of branches plus one
questions.explanation: Glycogen is the multibranched polysaccharide of glucose molecules in which a large number of glucose monosaccharides are bonded with each other through 1 to 4 and 1,6 glycosidic linkage. The number of ends in a glycogen molecule would be equal to the number of branches plus one.

questions.question: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.question_raw: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.explanation: Mangifera Indica. Here, Mangifera is a generic name while Indica is a specific name. Hence, the correct option is the Genus species.

questions.question: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.question_raw: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.explanation: The clear zone surrounding the Golgi apparatus is a zone of exclusion, where no other cellular activity takes place.

questions.question: Archegoniophore is found in ________.
questions.question_raw: Archegoniophore is found in ________.
questions.explanation: Archegoniophore (female gametophytes ) develop on the thallus of Marchantia. Each archegonium produces a single egg cell which helps in sexual reproduction.

questions.question: Which one of the following does not belonging to Urochordata?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following does not belonging to Urochordata?
questions.explanation: Lancelets are classified in the phylum Chordata, subphylum Cephalochordata. lancelet, the name for small, fishlike lower chordate also called amphioxus it shows many affinities with the vertebrates. There are about 30 lancelet species, most belonging to the genus Branchiostoma (formerly Amphioxus). Lancelets are usually about 1 in. (2.5 cm) long, with transparent bodies tapered at both ends. There is no distinct head and no paired fins.

questions.question: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included under the kingdom:
questions.question_raw: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included under the kingdom:
questions.explanation: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included in the kingdom Monera. The Eukaryotic unicellular organisms are placed in the Protista kingdom.

questions.question: The junctions which help to stop substances from leaking across a tissue is:
questions.question_raw: The junctions which help to stop substances from leaking across a tissue is:
questions.explanation: Tight junctions hold cells together. They are narrow belts that circle around the upper part of lateral surfaces of the adjacent epithelial cells to create a fusion point. They stop substances from leaking across a tissue as they prevent the passage of molecules and ions through space between adjacent cells.

questions.question: Animal cell does not possess:
questions.question_raw: Animal cell does not possess:
questions.explanation: An animal cell does not possess plasmodesmata but they contain ribosomes, centriole, and plasmalemma. Plasmodesmata are the connection between two plant cells.

questions.question: The exoskeleton of arthropods have ______.
questions.question_raw: The exoskeleton of arthropods have ______.
questions.explanation: Exoskeletons of arthropods have a complex polysaccharide called chitin. These complex polysaccharides are heteropolymers.

questions.question: Which of the following scientists started the practice of using biological names?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following scientists started the practice of using biological names?
questions.explanation: Carl Linnaeus (1707-1778) Carl Linnaeus, also known as Carl von Linné or Carolus Linnaeus, is often called the Father of Taxonomy. His system for naming, ranking, and classifying organisms is still in wide use today (with many changes).

questions.question: Association between Mycobiont and Phycobiont are found in
questions.question_raw: Association between Mycobiont and Phycobiont are found in
questions.explanation: In a lichen; the fungal component is called mycobiont and algal component is called phycobiont.

questions.question: A largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body is______.
questions.question_raw: A largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body is______.
questions.explanation: Spleen is the largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body. Blood formation in the foetus and newly born child takes place in spleen.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?
 The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
 Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
 Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?

  1. The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
  2. Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
  3. Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.explanation: All of these are the universal rules of binomial nomenclature. Hence, the correct option is All of these.

questions.question: Which of the following statements with reference to the cytoskeleton is not correct?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements with reference to the cytoskeleton is not correct?

options.text: Cytoskeleton maintains the shape of the cell
Cytoskeleton helpful in production of ATP

correct_option_texts: Cytoskeleton helpful in production of ATP
questions.explanation: The cytoskeleton in a cell is involved in functions like mechanical support, motility, maintenance of the shape of the cell.

questions.question: Diploid sporophyte is the dominant phase in the life cycle of which plant?
questions.question_raw: Diploid sporophyte is the dominant phase in the life cycle of which plant?
questions.explanation: Angiosperm exhibits a diplontic life cycle where diploid sporophyte represents the dominant phase.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 (i) Operculum
 (a) Ctenophora
 (ii) Parapodia
 (b) Mollusca
 (iii) Scales
 (c) Porifera
 (iv) Comb plates
 (d) Reptilia
 (v) Radula
 (e) Annelida
 (vi) Hairs
 (f) Cyclostomata and Chondrichthyes
 (vii) Choanocytes
 (g) Mammalia
 (viii) Gill slits
 (h) Osteichthyes
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

(i) Operculum

(a) Ctenophora

(ii) Parapodia

(b) Mollusca

(iii) Scales

(c) Porifera

(iv) Comb plates

(d) Reptilia

(v) Radula

(e) Annelida

(vi) Hairs

(f) Cyclostomata and Chondrichthyes

(vii) Choanocytes

(g) Mammalia

(viii) Gill slits

(h) Osteichthyes

questions.question: Which one of the following types of cell is involved in making of the inner walls of blood vessels?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following types of cell is involved in making of the inner walls of blood vessels?
questions.explanation: Endothelium is the single layer of the squamous epithelium lining the inner wall of blood vessels.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The flower is the reproductive unit in the angiosperms. It is meant for sexual reproduction. A typical flower has four different kinds of whorls arranged successively on the swollen end of the stalk or pedicel, called thalamus or receptacle. These are calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. Calyx and corolla are accessory organs, while androecium and gynoecium are reproductive organs. In symmetry, the flower may be actinomorphic (radial symmetry) or zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry). Based on the position of calyx, corolla and androecium in respect of the ovary on the thalamus, the flowers are described as hypogynous, perigynous and epigynous. A flower may be trimerous, tetramerous or pentamerous when the floral appendages are in multiple of 3, 4 or 5, respectively.
In some flowers like ________ the colyx and corolla are not distinct and term as perianth.
questions.question_raw:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

The flower is the reproductive unit in the angiosperms. It is meant for sexual reproduction. A typical flower has four different kinds of whorls arranged successively on the swollen end of the stalk or pedicel, called thalamus or receptacle. These are calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. Calyx and corolla are accessory organs, while androecium and gynoecium are reproductive organs. In symmetry, the flower may be actinomorphic (radial symmetry) or zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry). Based on the position of calyx, corolla and androecium in respect of the ovary on the thalamus, the flowers are described as hypogynous, perigynous and epigynous. A flower may be trimerous, tetramerous or pentamerous when the floral appendages are in multiple of 3, 4 or 5, respectively.


In some flowers like ________ the colyx and corolla are not distinct and term as perianth.
questions.explanation: lily

Which of the following is the incorrect match?
questions.explanation: Perianth-chilli

Assertion (A): In flowers of guava the ovary is half inferior. 
Reason (R): In the hypogynous flower the gynoecium occupies the highest position while the other parts are situated below it.

Which of the following statements is incorrect?
 Flowers with bracts-reduced leaf found at the base of the pedicel.
 When a flower has both androecium and gynoecium, it is unisexual.
 In plum ovary here is said to be half inferior.
 In epigynous flowers the ovary is superior.

Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

A typical flower has four different kinds of whorls arranged successively on the swollen end of the stalk or pedicel, called thalamus or receptacle. These are calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. Calyx and corolla are accessory organs, while androecium and gynoecium are reproductive organs. In symmetry, the flower may be actinomorphic (radial symmetry) or zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry). Based on the position of calyx, corolla and androecium in respect of the ovary on the thalamus, the flowers are described as hypogynous, perigynous and epigynous. A flower may be trimerous, tetramerous or pentamerous when the floral appendages are in multiple of 3, 4 or 5, respectively.
Which of the following represent epigynous?
questions.question_raw:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

The flower is the reproductive unit in the angiosperms. It is meant for sexual reproduction. A typical flower has four different kinds of whorls arranged successively on the swollen end of the stalk or pedicel, called thalamus or receptacle. These are calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. Calyx and corolla are accessory organs, while androecium and gynoecium are reproductive organs. In symmetry, the flower may be actinomorphic (radial symmetry) or zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry). Based on the position of calyx, corolla and androecium in respect of the ovary on the thalamus, the flowers are described as hypogynous, perigynous and epigynous. A flower may be trimerous, tetramerous or pentamerous when the floral appendages are in multiple of 3, 4 or 5, respectively.


Which of the following represent epigynous?

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question:
Match the following and choose the correct option:
 Column I
 Column II
 a. Touch
 i. Nasal epithelium
 b. Smell
 ii. Foramen magnum
 c. Cranial nerves
 iii. Sensory papillae
 d. Medulla oblongata
 iv. Peripheral nervous system
questions.question_raw:
Match the following and choose the correct option:

Column I Column II
a. Touch i. Nasal epithelium
b. Smell ii. Foramen magnum
c. Cranial nerves iii. Sensory papillae
d. Medulla oblongata iv. Peripheral nervous system
questions.explanation: Touch is sensory papillae, smell is the nasal epithelium, cranial nerves is a part of the peripheral nervous system, medulla oblongata is foramen magnum.

questions.question: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.question_raw: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.explanation: The first man to witness a live cell under a microscope was Anton van Leeuwenhoek, who in 1674 described the algae Spirogyra. Van Leeuwenhoek probably also saw bacteria.

questions.question: Primary metabolites are always found in animal cells. Which of the following is not an example of a primary metabolite?
questions.question_raw: Primary metabolites are always found in animal cells. Which of the following is not an example of a primary metabolite?
questions.explanation: Primary metabolites include energy-rich fuel molecules, such as sucrose and starch, structural components such as cellulose, informational molecules such as DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid), and pigments, such as chlorophyll.

questions.question: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.question_raw: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.explanation: The middle lamella is a layer mainly of calcium pectate which holds or glues the different neighbouring cells together.

questions.question: Which helps in transporting glucose into cells?
questions.question_raw: Which helps in transporting glucose into cells?
questions.explanation: Glut-4 enables glucose transport into cells.
